sprint: The Gate Covers the Full Screen
task: Direct, immediate follow-up on the just-shipped instant-start work: "the gate needs to cover the full screen." Until now .gateway-gates-wrap was still only a small, aspect-ratio-locked box (min(42vw,560px) at rest, min(94vw, 94vh*aspect) even at the Arrival climax) -- a real gap between what the sequence claimed ("Let the gate be there") and what it actually showed, since a Story Meadow-scale portion of the screen around the gate stayed the plain ambient sky. Root-caused, not patched around: .gateway-panel (the flex child holding all Gate/Lumo/greeting content) never actually reached the viewport's full height, since .gateway-overlay's own flex layout used align-items:center -- shrinking .gateway-panel to its own content's min-height (280px) rather than stretching it to fill the fixed, inset:0 .gateway-overlay above it. Fixed at that root: align-items:center became align-items:stretch (the flex container's only real flex-participating child, since .gateway-scene is position:absolute and never joins flex layout at all), giving .gateway-panel a genuine, definite 100%-of-viewport height for the first time. .gateway-gates-wrap was rewritten from a bottom-anchored, width/aspect-ratio-locked floating box into a true position:absolute; inset:0; width:100%; height:100%; backdrop layer, with .gateway-gate-video's object-fit switched from contain to cover so the real footage fills every edge regardless of viewport aspect ratio, cropping rather than letterboxing. The old floating-postcard chrome on .gateway-gate-video-frame (border-radius, an outer rim glow box-shadow, a radial-gradient edge-fade mask -- all designed for a bounded box that would otherwise look pasted onto the sky) is removed outright, since it would instead have punched a transparent hole in the corners of a true full-bleed layer; replaced with a gentle inset vignette (a pure darkening, no transparency) to keep the cinematic feel. Journey/Arrival's old width/bottom/top/height growth transitions -- which used to grow the box from its small resting size toward near-fullscreen -- are replaced with plain, centered transform:scale() escalations (1.0 -> 1.06 -> 1.16) layered on top of the now-always-full-bleed frame: since scaling a full-bleed, object-fit:cover video up only crops in further and can never reveal an edge, this still reads as "drawing closer, then arriving" while never shrinking below full coverage at any beat. .gateway-gate-halo (the Awaken-pulse glow) is moved to paint IN FRONT of the video frame instead of behind it (a real, found-while-verifying regression: once the video frame became opaque and full-bleed, a halo painted behind it in the original DOM order would have become permanently invisible, silently breaking the existing Awaken pulse effect) via one added z-index. The Story Egg (.gateway-egg-wrap, a percentage-positioned child of .gateway-gates, unchanged CSS) automatically inherits the new full-viewport sizing with no code change of its own, now tucked at the real screen's bottom-left rather than a smaller box's. The obsolete mobile-only .gateway-gates-wrap width override (min(56vw,280px)) is removed as dead weight now that full-bleed coverage applies uniformly at every viewport size. Verified via a new 13-assertion Playwright suite measuring real getBoundingClientRect() geometry (not just class presence) at rest, mid-Journey, and at Arrival -- confirming the Gate wrap and its inner video frame both genuinely span 0,0 to the full viewport at rest, that .gateway-panel itself is truly viewport-tall, that Lumo stays visibly on-screen in front of the Gate at every zoom level including the massive Arrival scale, and that the same full-bleed coverage holds at a real 390x844 mobile viewport too -- plus real screenshots at every beat confirming the composition reads well (the doors now genuinely dominate the frame, Lumo stands guard clearly on the right, the Egg is small and dim at bottom-left, and the Awaken beat's halo glow/magic sparks/Egg brightening are all still clearly visible layered atop the video). Full regression across every existing Gateway suite (the 21-assertion Traveller-path suite, the 7-assertion Returning-Creator-path suite, the tap-to-skip sanity check) passes unchanged, plus both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this sprint touches zero Builder files). No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to css/style.css, with a matching cache-bust version bump on both stylesheet <link> tags in index.html.
